articles.title: Unpacked: How agentic AI is reshaping the consumer journey

articles.summary: This week, I checked in on deodorant brand Curie’s new look and how it fits into Walmart’s demand for more premium beauty. Additionally, fragrance influencer Funmi Monet launches her own perfume line, and Macy’s plucks a former Kering exec to take over Bluemercury.
articles.title: Beauty Briefing: With aluminum-free deodorants now commonplace, Curie wants to be known as a performance brand

articles.summary: Beyond Yoga's overall sales grew 14% year over year in the last quarter, while direct revenue was up 40%. The newer categories, outside of the brand's original core product of yoga pants, leggings and sports bras, are now driving over 60% of Beyond Yoga's total revenue.
articles.title: ‘An evolution of the brand’: Lifestyle categories are now driving the majority of Beyond Yoga’s revenue

articles.summary: On Thursday, Sam's Club hosted its first-ever Sam's Wellness Club, an exclusive event bringing together creators, media and merchants at a loft event space overlooking Midtown Manhattan. Here's why the club retailer is so keen on promoting its health and wellness offerings right now.
articles.title: Sam’s Club takes Manhattan for wellness event with creators

articles.summary: Glossy sat down with Oura chief marketing officer Doug Sweeny on the eve of the FIFA World Cup finale weekend to unpack the company’s biggest marketing bet yet. Oura rolled out several campaigns and partnerships over the past few months tied to the tournament, including the announcement of England co-captains Harry Kane and Declan Rice as ambassadors, partnership with U.S. Soccer to become the official wearable of Team USA, and much more. Now, the company will parlay these “over-performing” camp
articles.title: Inside Oura’s winning bet on the World Cup — and what it means for the brand’s 2027 roadmap

articles.summary: Two years ago, fragrance generated around 20% of The Nue Co.’s sales. This year, the category is expected to account for 85% of total company net sales, as the wellness company redirects its inventory, marketing and product pipeline around its fastest-growing business.
articles.title: The Nue Co. expects fragrance to reach 85% of sales as Ulta fuels growth

articles.summary: Pink wants to be the first stop on a Gen-Z shopper’s trip to the mall this holiday season. To get there, the Victoria’s Secret-owned brand is expanding beyond the pajamas, fleece and intimates it is best known for, using accessories, denim, beauty and collaborations to position itself as a broader lifestyle brand for 18- to 24-year-old women.
articles.title: Victoria’s Secret Pink’s Gen-Z-focused holiday strategy, with president Ali Dillon

articles.summary: Coming off the success of “Off Campus,” Ella Bright earned another debut: On Thursday, the 19-year-old actress was announced as Rare Beauty’s first celebrity ambassador.
articles.title: Glossy Pop Newsletter: Why Rare Beauty is taking a ‘story-first’ approach to celebrity ambassadors

articles.summary: In this week’s Luxury Briefing, Glossy looks at how contemporary womenswear brand Toccin is using private shopping events, trunk shows and full-look selling to fuel its expansion. Also, what Richemont’s latest results signal about the widening divide within luxury. Plus, executive moves at Bottega Veneta, Balmain and La Perla, and other industry news to know.
articles.title: Luxury Briefing: Private shopping is powering Toccin’s international expansion

articles.summary: In recent years, the sneaker release schedule has been cluttered with new drops. But now, sneaker brands are taking a different approach: taking time between their releases. They're also debuting entirely new silhouettes, rather than repeatedly rolling out iterations on existing models. The aim is to increase the impact of each release as consumer interest in sneakers declines.
articles.title: Fashion Briefing: Sneaker brands are slowing their drops to combat sneaker fatigue

articles.summary: Longtime industry disruptor Gregg Renfrew joins the Glossy Beauty Podcast to discuss her new Gen Z-focused brand Beecee, plus answer questions about the future of clean beauty, what it takes to court young consumers and the future of specialty beauty retail.
articles.title: Gregg Renfrew is courting Gen Z with Beecee, but do young people care about clean beauty?

articles.summary: On Wednesday, Salomon officially launched in Foot Locker stores and online. Foot Locker representatives called the launch one of the retailer's "most exciting brand partnership moments of the year."
articles.title: Salomon continues its US growth streak by launching at Foot Locker

articles.summary: According to a report from 5W AI Communications, skin-care brands like The Ordinary, Cerave and La Roche-Posay are among the top beauty brands by AI citations. Those results speak to a new discovery model where LLMs like ChatGPT and Google Gemini prioritize ingredients and expert credentials to surface product recommendations. But the AI beauty pipeline still remains undefined.
articles.title: Ingredient-led brands are dominating AI beauty citations

articles.summary: For the Wellness Briefing, Glossy interviewed Jenny Duan, the 22-year-old founder who has raised $11.6 million to launch a continuous hormone-tracking wearable named Clair. It’s part of the burgeoning hormone tracking marketplace that could soon upend how women interact with wearables.
articles.title: Wellness Briefing: Is hormone tracking the future of women’s wearables? Plus news

articles.summary: This week, I checked in on Urban Outfitters’ growing beauty assortment and Coty’s decision to send Gucci Beauty back to Kering. Additionally, Bobbi Brown’s son steps down as CEO of Jones Road, and Sol de Janeiro targets the male fragrance market.
articles.title: Beauty Briefing: Urban Outfitters doubles down on Gen Zalpha beauty, and Coty offloads Gucci ahead of schedule

articles.summary: "Love Island USA" has become a cultural phenomenon that many brands can no longer ignore. Ad sales were up 73% across the hit show this season, and "Love Island" has now become an integral part of summer-long marketing campaigns for brands like Poppi.
articles.title: Brands won this season of ‘Love Island USA’

articles.summary: Advertisers are embracing AI for social media and retail media marketing. However, they are slower to adopt AI for influencer and CTV marketing. This is according to a Glossy+ Research survey of more than 100 marketing professionals in first-quarter 2026.
articles.title: Glossy+ Research: Marketers hesitate to adopt AI for influencer and CTV marketing

articles.summary: Glossy caught up with Milani CEO Mary van Praag for insights into her winning leadership strategy, including the power of product collabs with retailers and manufacturers, the categories and trends she’s betting on for the second half of the year, and the consumer demands from the mass category now.
articles.title: Milani CEO Mary van Praag unpacks the strategies behind 19 quarters of growth, trends she’s betting on for 2H

articles.summary: Lily Balaisis, the 18-year-old founder of Pink Palm Puff, said the brand has always intended to resonate with young girls. But now, Pink Palm Puff is ready to grow its customer base beyond high schoolers.
articles.title: How fast-growing Pink Palm Puff aims to expand beyond its core teen demographic

articles.summary: As the creator economy has grown, influencers and content creators have monetized their platforms by launching brands, to varying degrees of success. Now, they’re adding C-suite roles to their resumes.
